test_text.title: Healthcare Options and Talks in the Area

audioscript.text: Section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a man asking for information about health services in the place where he is living.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 1 to 6.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will see that there is an example that has been done for you.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: On this occasion only, the conversation relating to this will be played first.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, I've just moved to this area with my wife and children, and I'd like to know where we can all register with a doctor at a health center.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, there's Doctor Green at the Harvey clinic.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The woman says the doctor at the Harvey clinic is called Doctor Green.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: So, Green has been written in the space.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now we shall begin.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You should answer the questions as you listen, because you will not hear the recording a second time.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Listen carefully and answer questions 1 to 6.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Can I help you?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, I've just moved to this area with my wife and children, and I'd like to know where we can all register with a doctor at a health center.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Well, there's Doctor Green at the Harvey clinic.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We always recommend her for babies, because she's very good with them, and she runs a special clinic.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, actually my youngest child is 5, so that wouldn't be any good for us.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Right.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Is there anywhere else I could try?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, the Eshcol health practice is the next one on my list.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: How do you spell that?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: ESHCOL.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And it's Doctor Fuller who has space on his list.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The clinic only opened a year ago, so the facilities are all very modern.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That sounds good.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And it's particularly good if you're busy during the day, because they also do appointments in the evening.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: They're closed on Saturday though.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The only other place on the list is the health center on Shore Lane.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: You can register with Doctor Gormley, uh, that's GORMLEY.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: He's new there, but the center has a very good reputation.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, yes, I think I know the road.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That would be the best one.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Thanks, uh, could you tell me will all their services be free?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There are usually some small charges that doctors make.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Let me see what it says about the Shore Lane Center.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: If you need to be vaccinated before any trips abroad, you won't have to pay for this.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What else?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: The Sports Injury Treatment service operates on a paying basis.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: As does the nutritional therapy service.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Some health centers do offer alternative therapies like homeopathy as part of their pay-to-use service.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Shore Lane are hoping to do this soon.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I think they may start with acupuncture.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And finally, if you need to prove you're healthy, or haven't had any serious injuries before a new employer will accept you, you can get a free fitness check-up there.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: But you'd most likely have to pay for insurance medicals though.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: OK, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 7 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 7 to 10.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You might also be interested to know the center is running a pilot scheme of talks for patients.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I've got the list here, actually they look very interesting.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What sort of things?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Well, the first one's about giving up smoking.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It's next week, February 25th at 7 pm, and that's in room 4.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It says the talk will stress the health benefits particularly for people with asthma or heart disease.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That sounds very interesting.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's also a talk for families with children, it's on healthy eating, and takes place on the first of March at 5:00.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Will that be at the health center?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Actually it's at the primary school on Shore Lane.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: I imagine they're inviting the parents of pupils there.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It says here all welcome.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Hmm, I might go to that if I have time.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's a couple of other talks, one giving advice about how to avoid injuries while doing exercise.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It's on the ninth of March, oh, it's a late afternoon talk at 4:30 and it'll be in room 6.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: It also says the talk is suitable for all ages.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: And finally there's a talk called 'Stress Management' which is...
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 1.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

body.title: Which TWO of the following are offered free of charge at Shore Lane Health Centre?

desc.constraint: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER

items: useful for people with asthma or <input> problems
body.title: Talks for patients at Shore Lane Health Centre

audioscript.text: Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a man who owns a holiday home, talking on the phone to a woman who is staying there.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully, and answer questions 11 to 13.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hi, it's Laura Carlton here.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: We've just arrived at the holiday flat, but I can't get the hot water and heating to work.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Oh, right.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's easy.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Don't worry.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: In the upstairs cupboard, you'll find the water heater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You'll see three main controls on the left at the bottom of the heater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The first one, the round one on the far left, is the most important one for the heating and hot water.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It's the main control switch.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Make sure it's in the on position.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The switch itself doesn't light up, but the little square below will be black if the switch is off.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Uh. That's probably what's happened.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It's got switched off by mistake.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The middle one of these three controls, you'll see it's slightly larger than the first one, controls the radiators.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: If you feel cold while you're there, and need the radiators on, this needs to be turned to maximum.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The last of the three controls, the one on the right, is usually on about a No. 4 setting, which for the water in the taps is usually quite hot enough.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Below the heating controls in the middle is a small round plastic button.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: If there isn't enough water in the pipes, sometimes the heater goes out.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: If this happens, you'll need to press this button to reset the heater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hold it in for about 5 seconds, and the heater should come on again.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Then there's a little square indicator under the third knob that's a kind of alarm light.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It'll flash if you need to reset the heater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Hmm. It sounds complicated.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Ha, I'm sure you won't have any problems with it.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There should be some more instructions on the side of the heater.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Call me back if you can't make it work.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: OK.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 14 to 20.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: While you're on the phone, we haven't managed to find a few things we need, like extra pillows for the beds and some washing powder.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Is there any here?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Pillows, uh, yes.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: If you look in the cupboard, the large white one upstairs, to the left of the bathroom door, there should be 4 or 5 on the top shelf.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And if you want to do some washing, there's some powder for that.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Um. Ha, probably by the back door.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's a kind of shelf there above the sink.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: In fact, I'm sure there's some there in a large blue box.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You need about half a cupful for each wash.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Oh, and that reminds me, the spare key to the back door is hanging on a hook on the wall by the sitting room window.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Please make sure to put it back when you've used it.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The previous guests lost it in the garden, and I had to get another one made.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: And if you have any trouble with the lamps, you'll find some spare bulbs in a large cardboard box.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It's on top of the washing machine with all kinds of useful things in it.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Oh, and another thing I forgot to mention when we last spoke.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Yes, I've left you a local map.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: So you'll be able to find your way around easily.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It shows the whole area.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I put it in the top drawer of the chest under the TV in your bedroom.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: There's a whole file of local information in there too.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Thanks.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: What about visiting the town?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Can you give us any advice?
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Yes, you'll need to take the car.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It's too far to walk from the flat, really.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You have to pay to leave your car in all the car parks now, I'm afraid.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: I like the one that's by the station best, and you can walk to the town center from there in 5 minutes.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: That's where all the best restaurants are, but if you want a takeaway, the Italian one does really good pasta and pizzas.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Call 7 3 22 8 1 for that one.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Or 7 66 11 9 for the Chinese.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: They're both good, and they'll both deliver to the flat.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: As for places to visit yes, do go and see the railway museum.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: The exhibition is small, but really good.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: It gets very crowded on Sundays, so I suggest you visit it on a quieter day later in the week.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: But not on Thursdays, which is market day.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: You won't find anywhere to park, and it's also the only day of the week when they're not open.
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Anything else?
audioscript.speaker: MAN

audioscript.text: Not for the moment.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: Thanks.
audioscript.speaker: WOMAN

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 2.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: You need about half a cupful for each wash.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Oh, and that reminds me, the spare key to the back door is hanging on a hook on the wall by the sitting room window.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Please make sure to put it back when you've used it.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: The previous guests lost it in the garden, and I had to get another one made.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: And if you have any trouble with the lamps, you'll find some spare bulbs in a large cardboard box.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: It's on top of the washing machine with all kinds of useful things in it.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Oh, and another thing I forgot to mention when we last spoke.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Yes, I've left you a local map.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: So you'll be able to find your way around easily.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: It shows the whole area.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: I put it in the top drawer of the chest under the TV in your bedroom.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: There's a whole file of local information in there too.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Thanks.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: What about visiting the town?
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Can you give us any advice?
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Yes, you'll need to take the car.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: It's too far to walk from the flat, really.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: You have to pay to leave your car in all the car parks now, I'm afraid.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: I like the one that's by the station best, and you can walk to the town center from there in 5 minutes.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: That's where all the best restaurants are, but if you want a takeaway, the Italian one does really good pasta and pizzas.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Call 7 3 22 8 1 for that one.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Or 7 66 11 9 for the Chinese.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: They're both good, and they'll both deliver to the flat.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: As for places to visit yes, do go and see the railway museum.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: The exhibition is small, but really good.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: It gets very crowded on Sundays, so I suggest you visit it on a quieter day later in the week.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: But not on Thursdays, which is market day.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: You won't find anywhere to park, and it's also the only day of the week when they're not open.
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Anything else?
utterances.speaker: MAN

utterances.text: Not for the moment.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: Thanks.
utterances.speaker: WOMAN

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 2.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

text: Where can each of the following items be found?
textReadable: Where can each of the following items be found?

list: next to window in living room

items: The best place to park in town - next to the station

test_text.title: Kira's University Study Experience and Insights

audioscript.text: Section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a conversation between an English teacher called Paul, and a former student of his called Kira.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 21 to 25.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Hello, Kira.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: How are you?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Fine, thanks, Paul.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: How are you?
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Well, thanks.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: It's good to see you.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: It must be 12 months since you did our course.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: That's right.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: It's nice to come back and say hello.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: What course did you enroll in?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Actually, I went straight into 3rd year pharmacy.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: They credited me with 2 years which probably made it more difficult for me.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: On the other hand, you were lucky to be granted credits.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Is that why you chose the course?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Yes, and as I'd already finished a course in it in my country, I thought it would be easier if I studied something I already knew.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: I didn't realize you went into 3rd year.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: I thought you started in first year.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: No wonder it was so hard.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: And what do you think is one of the big differences between studying at a university here and studying in your country?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Well, I found it very difficult to write assignments.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Because I wasn't familiar with that aspect of the system here.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: The main problem is that the lecturers expect you to be critical.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: That made me feel really terrible.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: I thought "How can I possibly do it? How can I comment on someone else's research when they probably spent 5 years doing it?"
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: I think a lot of people who come from overseas countries have similar problems, but after a while, it became easier for me.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: People expect you to have problems with the process of reading and writing, but in fact, it is more a question of altering your viewpoint towards academic study.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Hmm, uh, how was the content of the lectures?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Was it easy for you?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: I didn't really have many problems understanding lectures.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: The content was very similar to what I'd studied before.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: And what about the lecturers themselves?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Are they essentially the same as lecturers in your country?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Uh. Well, actually no.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Here they're much easier to approach.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: After every lecture, you can go and ask them something you didn't understand, or you can make an appointment and talk to them about anything in the course.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Maybe you found them different because you're a more mature student now, whereas when you were studying in your country, you were younger and not so assertive.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: No, I don't think that's the difference.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Most of the students here do it.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: In my faculty, they all seem to make appointments, usually to talk about something in the course that's worrying them, but sometimes just about something that might really interest them.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Something they might want to specialize in.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Hmm. The lecturers must set aside certain times every week when they're available for students.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: That's good to hear.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Before you hear the rest of the conversation, you have some time to look at questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen and answer questions 26 to 30.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: And how was your timetable?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Was it a very busy year?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Oh, very very busy.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: They make you work very hard.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Apart from lectures, we had practical sessions in a lot of subjects.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: We did these in small groups.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: I had to go and work 4 hours every week in a community pharmacy.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Hmm. Actually, I enjoyed this very much, meeting new people all the time.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Then in 2nd semester, we had to get experience in hospital dispensaries.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: So every second day we went to one of the big hospitals and worked there.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: And on top of all that we had our assignments, which took me a lot of time.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Oh, I nearly forgot, between 1st and 2nd semesters, we had to work full time for two weeks in a hospital.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: That does sound a very heavy year.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: So are you pleased now that you did it?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Do you feel some sense of achievement?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Yeah, I do feel much more confident, which I suppose is the most important thing.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: And have you got any recommendations for people who are studying from overseas?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Well, I suppose they need very good English.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: It would be much better if they spent more time learning English before they enter the university.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Because you can be in big trouble if you don't understand what people are saying, and you haven't got time to translate.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: Uh. Anything else?
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Well, as I said before.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: The biggest problem for me was lack of familiarity with the education system here.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: It sounds as if it was a real challenge.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Congratulations, Kira.
audioscript.speaker: PAUL

audioscript.text: Hmm, thanks, Paul.
audioscript.speaker: KIRA

audioscript.text: That is the end of section 3.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

test_text.title: Wildlife in City Gardens: Project Findings and Insights

audioscript.text: Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You will hear a talk about a project on the wildlife found in city gardens in Britain.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: First, you have some time to look at questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: Good morning. Today I'd like to present the findings of our Year 2 project on wildlife found in gardens throughout our city.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: I'll start by saying something about the background to the project.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Then talk a little bit about our research techniques, and then indicate some of our interim findings.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: First of all, how did we choose our topic?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, there are four of us in the group, and one day while we were discussing a possible focus,
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: two of the group mentioned that they had seen yet more sparrow hawks - one of Britain's most interesting birds of prey - in their own city center gardens, and wondered why they were turning up in these gardens in great numbers.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We were all very engaged by the idea of why wild animals would choose to inhabit a city garden.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Why is it so popular with wildlife when the countryside itself is becoming less so?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The first thing we did was to establish what proportion of the urban land is taken up by private gardens.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We estimated that it was about 1/5, and this was endorsed by looking at large scale usage maps in the town land survey office.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: 24% to be precise.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our own informal discussions with neighbors and friends led us to believe that many garden owners had interesting experiences to relate regarding wild animal sightings.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So we decided to survey garden owners from different areas of the city.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Just over 100 of them completed a survey once every two weeks for 12 months.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Ticking off species they had seen from a pro forma list, and adding the names of any rarer ones.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Meanwhile, we were doing our own observations in selected gardens throughout the city.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We deliberately chose smaller ones because they were by far the most typical in the city.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The whole point of the project was to look at the norm, not the exception.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Alongside this primary research on urban gardens we were studying a lot of books about the decline of wild animals in the countryside, and thinking of possible causes for this.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So what did we find?
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Well, so much that I just won't have time to tell you about here.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: If you're interested in reading our more comprehensive findings, we've produced detailed graphic representations on the college website, and of course any of the group would be happy to talk to you about them.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Just email us.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: What we've decided to present today is information about just three species, because we felt these gave a good indication of the processes at work in rural and urban settings as a whole.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: The first species to generate a lot of interesting information was frogs, and there was a clear pattern here.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: They proliferate where there is suitable water, garden ponds are on the increase.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Rural ponds are disappearing, leading to massive migration to the towns.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Hedgehogs are also finding it easier to live in urban areas this time because their predators are not finding it quite so attractive to leave their rural environment.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: So hedgehogs have a better survival rate in cities.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: We had lots of sightings, so all in all we had no difficulties with our efforts to count their numbers precisely.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Our final species is the finest of bird singers, the song thrush.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: On the decline in the countryside, they are experiencing a resurgence in urban gardens, because these days gardeners are buying lots of different plants, which means there's an extensive range of seeds around, which is what they feed on.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Another factor is the provision of nesting places, which is actually better in gardens than the countryside.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Hard to believe it, but it's true.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Incidentally, we discovered that a massive new survey on song thrushes is about to be launched, so you should keep an eye open for that.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: Now, I'd be happy to answer any questions you may have.
audioscript.speaker: SPEAKER

audioscript.text: That is the end of Section 4.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

audioscript.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
audioscript.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Why is it so popular with wildlife when the countryside itself is becoming less so?
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The first thing we did was to establish what proportion of the urban land is taken up by private gardens.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We estimated that it was about 1/5, and this was endorsed by looking at large scale usage maps in the town land survey office.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: 24% to be precise.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Our own informal discussions with neighbors and friends led us to believe that many garden owners had interesting experiences to relate regarding wild animal sightings.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So we decided to survey garden owners from different areas of the city.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Just over 100 of them completed a survey once every two weeks for 12 months.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Ticking off species they had seen from a pro forma list, and adding the names of any rarer ones.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Meanwhile, we were doing our own observations in selected gardens throughout the city.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We deliberately chose smaller ones because they were by far the most typical in the city.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The whole point of the project was to look at the norm, not the exception.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Alongside this primary research on urban gardens we were studying a lot of books about the decline of wild animals in the countryside, and thinking of possible causes for this.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So what did we find?
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Well, so much that I just won't have time to tell you about here.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: If you're interested in reading our more comprehensive findings, we've produced detailed graphic representations on the college website, and of course any of the group would be happy to talk to you about them.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Just email us.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: What we've decided to present today is information about just three species, because we felt these gave a good indication of the processes at work in rural and urban settings as a whole.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: The first species to generate a lot of interesting information was frogs, and there was a clear pattern here.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: They proliferate where there is suitable water, garden ponds are on the increase.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Rural ponds are disappearing, leading to massive migration to the towns.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Hedgehogs are also finding it easier to live in urban areas this time because their predators are not finding it quite so attractive to leave their rural environment.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: So hedgehogs have a better survival rate in cities.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: We had lots of sightings, so all in all we had no difficulties with our efforts to count their numbers precisely.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Our final species is the finest of bird singers, the song thrush.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: On the decline in the countryside, they are experiencing a resurgence in urban gardens, because these days gardeners are buying lots of different plants, which means there's an extensive range of seeds around, which is what they feed on.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Another factor is the provision of nesting places, which is actually better in gardens than the countryside.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Hard to believe it, but it's true.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Incidentally, we discovered that a massive new survey on song thrushes is about to be launched, so you should keep an eye open for that.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: Now, I'd be happy to answer any questions you may have.
utterances.speaker: SPEAKER

utterances.text: That is the end of Section 4.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

utterances.text: You now have half a minute to check your answers.
utterances.speaker: ANNOUNCER

items.title: What led the group to choose their topic?
options: They were concerned about the decline of one species.
They were interested in the effects of city growth.
They wanted to investigate a recent phenomenon.

items.title: The exact proportion of land devoted to private gardens was confirmed by
The group asked garden owners to
options: keep a record of animals they saw.
get in contact when they saw a rare species.

items.title: The group made their observations in gardens
options: which had a large number of animal species.
which they considered to be representative.
which had stable populations of rare animals.

items.title: The group did extensive reading on
The speaker focuses on three animal species because
options: a lot of data has been obtained about them.
the group were most interested in them.

items: Reason for population increase in gardens
